memory_entries.text: In the product review SQLite benchmark, group g1 is the office products dataset (items_g1/fdbk_g1/taxn_g1/attrs_g1/fdbk_stats_g1); group g2 appears to be electronics; group g3 appears to be musical instruments. fdbk_g1.ts stores Unix timestamps in milliseconds, and dates from 2031-01-01 onward can be counted with ts >= strftime('%s','2031-01-01')*1000.
In the product review SQLite benchmark, for the previous office-products question 5 the correct answer was 2715 (my prior answer 0 was wrong); use feedback to calibrate future queries.

In the product review SQLite benchmark electronics dataset (g2), items_g2 has prices in prc_usd; top-level categories are in taxn_g2 with cat_lvl=1 (e.g., 'Computers & Accessories'), while items_g2.main_cat may use broader/different names like 'Computers'. For category price questions, join distinct items_g2.ref_id to taxn_g2 and filter prc_usd > 0.

In the product review SQLite benchmark, musical instruments are group g3; fdbk_g3.ts is stored as text dates ('YYYY-MM-DD'), not Unix milliseconds. For year-month grouping use substr(ts,1,7). For question 9 the most musical instrument reviews were in 2020-01 (4715 reviews).

In the product review SQLite benchmark musical instruments dataset (g3), items_g3 has columns ref_id, ttl, avg_rtg, rtg_ct, prc, str_nm, main_cat, desc_txt, but str_nm may be empty; product brand is in attrs_g3 where attr_key='Brand'. For question 11, the most common musical-instrument brand was Fender with 243 distinct products.

In the product review SQLite benchmark electronics dataset (g2), items_g2 has no review-count column; zero-review products can be identified with NOT EXISTS against fdbk_g2 on ref_id. For question 13, among zero-review electronics products, 3253 of 11345 had prc_usd non-NULL/non-zero, i.e. 28.67%.

In the product review SQLite benchmark electronics zero-review price question 13 correct answer was 29.93%, not my computed 28.67%; be careful with denominator/filter definitions for percentage questions.

In the product review SQLite benchmark office-products dataset (g1), product listing date is in attrs_g1 with attr_key='Date First Available'; values are mostly like 'April 1, 2014' plus some malformed month strings like '01M 10, 2009'. The year can be extracted as CAST(substr(trim(attr_val), -4) AS INTEGER). For question 20, office products listed before 2015 counted as COUNT(DISTINCT ref_id)=5254.

In the product review SQLite benchmark electronics dataset (g2), items_g2 columns include prc_usd (list price in dollars) and img_ct (catalog image count). For question 21, electronics with img_ct >= 1 and prc_usd > 0 had n=6344 and median list price $20.95.

In the product review SQLite benchmark musical instruments dataset (g3), attributes table may be named product_attributes_g3 rather than attrs_g3; same columns ref_id, attr_key, attr_val. For question 24, among brands with at least 50 distinct products with positive list prices, Fender had highest average list price (~$362.20) vs JIM DUNLOP.

In the product review SQLite benchmark musical instruments dataset (g3), current product attribute feed is product_attributes_g3 and older export is attrs_g3_legacy. For question 28, row-count ratio current/legacy was 202543/182566 = 1.11.

In the product review SQLite benchmark office-products dataset (g1), items_g1 has status TEXT DEFAULT 'active' with archived products no longer offered. fdbk_g1 has verified_status text labels ('verified','unverified',NULL). For question 29, among reviews missing text verification label (verified_status IS NULL/blank), 6912 of 113242 belonged to archived products = 6.1%.

In product review SQLite benchmark office-products dataset (g1), for active catalog products (items_g1.status='active') with non-empty review body, average body length was 182.0433 for missing verified_status vs 180.9509 for labeled reviews; question 30 answer unlabeled-labeled rounded to 1 decimal is 1.1.

In product review SQLite benchmark office-products dataset (g1), archived products are items_g1.status='archived' and fdbk_g1.verified_status='verified' denotes verified purchase. For question 31, archived office-product reviews had 15264 verified of 23016 total = 66.3%.

In product review SQLite benchmark office-products question 31, my previous computation for archived-product verified review share was wrong; correct answer is 94.8%, so future archived/verified questions may require stricter denominator or field interpretation than simply status='archived' with verified_status='verified'.
